description: Message utilities for zchat-channel-server. Provides three core capabilities: (1) @mention detection — checks whether a message body contains an @mention targeting a specific agent (message.py:12); (2) @mention cleaning — strips the @mention from a message and trims whitespace (message.py:17); (3) IRC-safe message chunking — splits long messages into chunks that fit within IRC's 512-byte-per-message limit (reserving ~120 bytes for IRC headers, yielding a 390-byte payload default), handling UTF-8 multi-byte characters (e.g., CJK at 3 bytes each) and sanitizing newlines to spaces (message.py:27). The module is a pure utility with no external dependencies beyond the Python standard library.

key_symbols.name: MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES
key_symbols.description: Default max byte payload per IRC message (390 bytes), after reserving ~120 bytes for IRC headers from the 512-byte RFC 2812 limit.

key_symbols.name: detect_mention
key_symbols.description: Check if a message body contains an @mention of the given agent name. Uses simple substring match on '@{agent_name}'.

key_symbols.name: clean_mention
key_symbols.description: Remove '@{agent_name}' from message body and strip surrounding whitespace.

key_symbols.name: _sanitize_for_irc
key_symbols.description: Private helper that replaces all newline variants (\r\n, \n, \r) with spaces for IRC single-line protocol compliance.

key_symbols.name: chunk_message
key_symbols.description: Split a message into a list of chunks that each fit within max_bytes (UTF-8 encoded). Sanitizes newlines first, then uses a conservative CJK-aware byte estimation loop and prefers breaking at spaces.

key_symbols.name: test_detect_mention
key_symbols.description: Tests @mention detection at start, middle, and absence of mention.

key_symbols.name: test_clean_mention
key_symbols.description: Tests that @mention is stripped and result is trimmed.

key_symbols.name: test_chunk_message_short
key_symbols.description: Tests that a short message returns a single-element list.

key_symbols.name: test_chunk_message_long
key_symbols.description: Tests chunking of a 5000-byte ASCII string into multiple chunks all <= 400 bytes.

key_symbols.name: test_chunk_message_cjk
key_symbols.description: Tests chunking with CJK characters (3 bytes each in UTF-8), verifying each chunk <= 390 bytes.

key_symbols.name: test_chunk_message_strips_newlines
key_symbols.description: Tests that newlines (\n, \r\n) are replaced with spaces in chunked output.

key_symbols.name: test_detect_mention_with_dash_separator
key_symbols.description: Tests that dash-separated agent names (IRC compliant) are detected but colon-separated names are not.

interfaces.name: chunk_message(text: str, max_bytes: int = MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES) -> list[str]

user_flows.name: mention_detection_and_extraction
steps: Incoming IRC PRIVMSG body is checked via detect_mention() for @agent_name substring (message.py:12)
If mention detected, clean_mention() strips the @mention and trims whitespace to produce the clean query text (message.py:17)

user_flows.name: outbound_message_chunking
steps: Outbound text is passed to chunk_message() (message.py:27)
_sanitize_for_irc() replaces all newline variants with spaces (message.py:22-24)
If UTF-8 byte length <= max_bytes, return single-element list (message.py:35-36)
Otherwise, iteratively find longest prefix fitting max_bytes using conservative CJK estimate (message.py:48-51)
Prefer breaking at space boundaries; fall back to hard byte-boundary cut (message.py:55-58)
Return list of chunks, each guaranteed <= max_bytes in UTF-8 (message.py:63)
